note: SNG-207 — the AUTHORITATIVE list of what the Repair panel / stateOps can actually do, for the GM's context. The GM references this so it can NEVER hallucinate a fix-screen control or deflect to a missing one. Built from corrections.js's real op set. If corrections.js gains/loses an op, this updates. The GM's rule: prefer emitting the op yourself in-turn; the panel is the player-facing fallback for the same ops, not a different capability.
theRule: The Repair panel exposes exactly these ops and no others. If a fix is not in this list, the panel CANNOT do it — do not send the player there for it. If a fix IS in this list, YOU can emit it in-turn — prefer that over deflecting.

ops.correctEntityField: Fix ANY wrong DESCRIPTIVE field on ANY entity (SNG-213): an NPC's name/role/description/status, a place's or quest's or item's or codex entry's text, a player field. Repair only — the general fix that closes the coverage gaps. Numeric/earned fields (rank/attribute/vital/relationship) are NOT here — they keep their own lower-only ops.
ops.correctField: Fix a wrongly-set character field: background, origin, nativeTradition, form. Repair only — a field never chosen or set wrong.
ops.correctDomain: Fix a domain slot (primary/secondary/tertiary) the game GUESSED wrong.
ops.removeEntity: Remove an entity never actually acquired: a companion, quest, codex fact, or NPC that shouldn't be there.
ops.unstickQuest: Free a quest stuck in a bad state so it can progress again.
ops.reanchorLocation: Repoint a desynced location pointer to where the character actually is.
ops.fixCodexFact: Correct or remove a wrong codex fact.
ops.correctAbilityRank: LOWER an ability rank set higher than practice earned. Only lowers — a raise is refused (power is earned through play).
ops.correctBond: Set a wrong relationship right (bondType/stage/relationship). A romantic bond on a minor is absolutely refused.
ops.correctVital: Re-sync a vital past its max. Current health/energy may only be LOWERED; a max may be corrected either way.
ops.correctAttribute: LOWER a mis-set sub-attribute; parents rederive. Only lowers.
ops.mergeEntity: Fold two split registry records for ONE person into a single survivor (unions history, keeps the higher relationship, adds the old name as an alias).

cannotDoHere: Grant xp, levels, skill points, or attunement — advancement is earned through play, not repaired in.
RAISE an ability rank, attribute, or current vital — only play raises these.
Create an item, ability, or NPC the story never conferred — that is authoring, not repair (the GM may GRANT what the fiction gave, in-turn, by its own fairness judgment; the panel does not).
Move minor-safety or content-rating floors — these are absolute and never adjustable by any op or panel.
